# Final cmd2mcpserver test with mcp-probe pattern
#
# This test shows how to use mcp-probe to test a generated MCP server

# Create a test program
-- hello/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

var name = flag.String("name", "world", "name to greet")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	fmt.Printf("Hello, %s!\n", *name)
}

-- hello/go.mod --
module github.com/test/hello

go 1.21

# Generate MCP server
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver hello
stdout 'Server generated successfully'

# Build the MCP server
cd mcpserver
exec go build -o hello-mcp
cd ..

# Create a probe script to initialize the server
-- init.jsonrpc --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"0.1.0","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"probe","version":"1.0"}},"id":1}

# Create a probe script to call tools/list
-- tools_list.jsonrpc --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":2}

# Initialize the server
stdin init.jsonrpc
exec ./mcpserver/hello-mcp
stdout '"jsonrpc":\s*"2.0"'
stdout '"result"'
stdout '"protocolVersion"'
stdout '"serverInfo"'

# Get the tools list
stdin tools_list.jsonrpc
exec ./mcpserver/hello-mcp
stdout '"jsonrpc":\s*"2.0"'
stdout '"result"'
stdout '"tools"'
stdout '"name":\s*"hello"'

# Verify the tool has proper input schema with our flags
stdout '"inputSchema"'
stdout '"properties"'
stdout '"name":\s*{[^}]*"type":\s*"string"'

# Clean up
exec rm -rf hello mcpserver
